title: A Taste Of Freedom

jokes.text: So, what is Freedom Day? Sounds like a feminine hygiene product.
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately makes a crude product comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - crude, direct, and efficient. Great economy but relies on easy crude humor

jokes.text: No, it's a fabulous, crab-ulous day!
jokes.setup: Defending Freedom Day from Bender's crude comment
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg makes up 'crab-ulous' as a portmanteau
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that's perfectly in character for Zoidberg's enthusiastic nature

jokes.text: If you want to do something, you do it, and just spleck with the consequences. You know, like how I live every day.
jokes.setup: Explaining the spirit of Freedom Day
jokes.punchline: Bender acts like this is special when it's literally his daily philosophy
jokes.explanation: Perfect character work - Bender completely missing that he already embodies this philosophy

jokes.text: Happy Freedom Day! Oh, I think my wrist is broken.
jokes.punchline: Immediate injury revelation
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy setup-punchline but lacks originality

jokes.text: Mm. That'll feel nice on my shattered bones.
jokes.punchline: Casually optimistic about hot tub helping 'shattered bones'
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered casually - the understatement makes it work

jokes.text: Wow, nude hot-tubbing? That's all I need to hear about Freedom Day.
jokes.setup: Complex explanation of Freedom Day's meaning
jokes.punchline: Fry reduces it to the nudity aspect
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry behavior - missing the deeper meaning for surface-level appeal

jokes.text: Sure, you can be a comedian instead of a doctor... if you want your parents to roll over in their graves!
jokes.setup: Explaining lack of freedom on his planet
jokes.punchline: Specific Jewish parent guilt about career choices
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor playing on Zoidberg's Jewish coding and family guilt

jokes.text: Sure, you can go to medical school... if you've given up on your dream of being a comedian!
jokes.setup: Previous example about comedian vs doctor
jokes.punchline: The reverse situation with the same guilt structure
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation that shows the no-win situation on his planet

jokes.text: That's why I love Earth. You can do what you want, and no one makes you feel guilty... because no one cares. We're not listening! That's what I'm talking about!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg explaining why Earth is better
jokes.punchline: Others immediately prove his point by not listening
jokes.explanation: Excellent execution - the immediate proof of his point makes it land perfectly

jokes.text: We're not listening! / That's what I'm talking about!
jokes.punchline: Crowd immediately proves his point by not listening
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate proof of the previous joke. Meta-humor that works on multiple levels.

jokes.text: Ow! You broke my foot! Freedom.
jokes.setup: Someone gets injured by Bender's 'freedom train'
jokes.punchline: Bender's casual 'Freedom' as justification
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - one word that encapsulates Bender's selfish philosophy

jokes.text: Come on, let loose and show me something. Anything. Seriously, I'd take an armpit.
jokes.setup: Zapp trying to get women to show him something
jokes.punchline: Escalating desperation down to 'an armpit'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp - the escalating desperation is perfectly in character

jokes.text: We're free to choose which hand our sex-monitoring chip is implanted in.
jokes.punchline: The 'freedom' is which hand gets the monitoring device
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dystopian humor - the illusion of choice in a surveillance state

jokes.text: If we don't want to pay our taxes... why, we're free to spend a weekend with the Pain Monster.
jokes.punchline: Tax evasion punishment is 'the Pain Monster'
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the dystopian freedom theme with a memorable monster name

jokes.text: See you April 15, folks!
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to Tax Day
jokes.explanation: Simple callback to real Tax Day - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: The great taste of Charleston Chew.
jokes.setup: Serious speech about what's truly important after hardships
jokes.punchline: Pivots to candy product placement
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - builds up something profound then deflates with product placement

jokes.text: I'm swelling with patriotic mucus.
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's gross biological take on 'swelling with pride'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg line - takes a common phrase and makes it disgustingly biological

jokes.text: In our darkest hour, we can stand erect with proud, up-thrust bosoms.
jokes.punchline: Nixon's weirdly sexual phrasing
jokes.explanation: Typical Nixon awkwardness - the sexual undertones are in character

jokes.text: Anyone who laughs is a Communist.
jokes.punchline: Threatens viewers who might laugh
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor - Nixon threatens the audience for reacting to the joke they just set up

jokes.text: Yes, fellow patriots! I ate your flag! And I did it with pride. For to express oneself by doing a thing, is the very essence of Freedom Day!
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg proudly admits to eating it as a freedom expression
jokes.explanation: Major plot moment with perfect character logic - Zoidberg's innocent interpretation leads to disaster

jokes.text: Kill him! Kill the traitor!
jokes.punchline: Immediate violent mob reaction
jokes.explanation: Predictable mob reaction - serviceable but not particularly clever

jokes.text: [ZOIDBERG MIMICKING THE THREE STOOGES]
jokes.punchline: Visual gag of Zoidberg doing Three Stooges moves
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy callback - relies on recognition rather than originality

jokes.text: I'm all scuttled out! What? My planet's embassy? They're paid to not kill me! Alley-oop!
jokes.punchline: His relief that the embassy is 'paid to not kill me'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Zoidberg's low expectations are perfectly in character

jokes.text: But I guess I don't belong here any more than I do on our crappy home planet. Sorry. What sorry? Our planet stinks. We all know it.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg apologizes for calling their planet crappy
jokes.punchline: Ambassador's casual agreement that their planet stinks
jokes.explanation: Nice subversion - expected diplomatic politeness replaced with honest agreement

jokes.text: What, no marshmallows? Well, let's storm the place! Without my prior knowledge.
jokes.punchline: Immediate escalation to violence over missing marshmallows
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - the diplomatic cover 'without my prior knowledge' makes it funnier

jokes.text: You may even find the image of it festering in his bowels offensive.
jokes.setup: Defending Zoidberg's right to free expression
jokes.punchline: Graphic description of the flag's current state
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out humor that serves the plot - the vivid imagery enhances the offense

jokes.text: Maybe so. But I know a place where the Constitution doesn't mean squat! The Supreme Court hereby accepts the case of Earth v. Zoidberg.
jokes.punchline: Suggests Supreme Court doesn't respect Constitution
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire - the irony of the Supreme Court not respecting the Constitution they're meant to interpret

jokes.text: You can eat my dog, You can eat my truck, But you eat my flag, And you're out of luck
jokes.punchline: Absurd priorities - dog and truck are expendable, flag is not
jokes.explanation: Good satire of country music patriotism - the absurd priorities are the joke

jokes.text: I was hating Zoidberg before it was cool.
jokes.punchline: Bender claims hipster credit for hating him first
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender line - combines his meanness with hipster culture parody

jokes.text: Posers! I was hating Zoidberg before it was cool.
jokes.punchline: Bender's hipster take on being anti-Zoidberg first
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work - perfectly captures Bender's need to be first/best at everything, even hatred

jokes.text: Name's Old Man Waterfall... but most folks just call me Old Man. I'll never remember that.
jokes.setup: Character introduces himself with a simple nickname
jokes.punchline: Fry can't remember even the simple version
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry stupidity - can't remember the simplified version of a name

jokes.text: Name a body part and a planet, and I've taken a bullet in it, on it.
jokes.setup: Claims to be veteran of many wars
jokes.punchline: Absurdly comprehensive injury claim
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of veteran bragging - the specificity makes it funnier

jokes.text: I'll never remember that.
jokes.punchline: Still finds it too complicated
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry stupidity moment. Good economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: Son, to me a robot's just a garbage can with sparks coming out it. The sparks keep me warm.
jokes.setup: Bender's confused reaction to Old Man
jokes.punchline: Old Man's dismissive view of robots, then personal admission
jokes.explanation: Great character establishment - dismissive but then reveals he benefits from robots

jokes.text: Or I would, if I hadn't lost my teeth and nails on Mars and Saturn, respectively.
jokes.setup: Claims he'll fight tooth and nail
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation - he lost those body parts in wars
jokes.explanation: Clever callback that literalizes a common phrase with his established character trait

jokes.text: No! There's no cocoa marshmallows! And every night, the rats eat a little more of my foot.
jokes.setup: Asked if he's okay in the embassy
jokes.punchline: Escalates from minor complaint to horrific reality
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from petty complaint to genuinely disturbing situation

jokes.text: I was hatched ready.
jokes.setup: Asked if ready to present case
jokes.punchline: Plays on 'born ready' with reptilian/bird characteristic
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective wordplay based on the character's species

jokes.text: He shut you up, O'Connor.
jokes.punchline: Justice's casual, unprofessional reaction
jokes.explanation: Simple humor from unprofessional behavior in formal setting

jokes.text: Court's kind of fun when it's not my ass on the line. Nachos?
jokes.punchline: Casual enjoyment and snack offering during serious trial
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - selfish enjoyment of others' misfortune with casual snack offer

jokes.text: No, you don't! Because I lost my real hand planting the flag when we took back Halley's comet.
jokes.setup: Shows hand, judge says 'Yes, I do'
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's prosthetic with absurd war story
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual misdirection - sets up expectation then reveals the truth with absurd backstory

jokes.text: I love it even more than I love my seven wives. That's right, I'm a polygamist. Boo!
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of polygamy, crowd disapproval
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and character establishment - the casual way he mentions it makes it funnier

jokes.text: Yet I would gladly eat a flag myself... had I not used my intestine as a rope to hoist a flag made of my own skin...
jokes.punchline: Absurdly graphic description of his own patriotic sacrifice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - the graphic imagery is both disturbing and absurdly patriotic, perfectly in character

jokes.text: Freedom such as polygamy. Boo! I rest my case.
jokes.punchline: Specific example that crowd disapproves of, unfazed reaction
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows his consistency despite crowd disapproval

jokes.text: Six to three? I beat the spread!
jokes.punchline: Bender treats it like sports betting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - reduces everything to gambling, completely inappropriate timing

jokes.text: She means you, you turkey of the sea.
jokes.setup: Confusion about who needs to apologize
jokes.punchline: Creative insult combining Zoidberg's species with Thanksgiving reference
jokes.explanation: Clever insult that's both species-specific and holiday-themed - very quotable

jokes.text: Also, in a rare double-whammy decision, the court finds polygamy constitutional. Boo! I can't wait to tell my husband! Boo!
jokes.punchline: Woman excited about telling husband she can have more husbands
jokes.explanation: Good ironic twist - woman's excitement about polygamy for her own benefit

jokes.text: Aha! Now the rubber band's on the other claw!
jokes.punchline: Species-specific variation of 'shoe's on the other foot'
jokes.explanation: Nice species-specific twist on common phrase - fits the crustacean character perfectly

jokes.text: Well, we'll do to you what we did to the Squash men of the Squash planet: Squished them!
jokes.setup: Threatening Earth with their military might
jokes.punchline: Species name perfectly matches their fate
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective pun - the perfect match of species name to defeat method

jokes.text: Charleston Chew? You bet!
jokes.punchline: Casual acceptance of product placement
jokes.explanation: Simple callback to earlier product placement - breaks tension with absurd priorities

jokes.text: Hugh Man, sir! Hugh Man. Now, that's a name I can trust.
jokes.punchline: Obviously fake name that Zapp completely buys
jokes.explanation: Classic spy name joke made funnier by Zapp's complete gullibility

jokes.text: You're damn right there is. That young lad's gunning for your job. And he just might get it.
jokes.setup: Kif trying to warn about suspicious ensign
jokes.punchline: Zapp completely misinterprets the warning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp moment - his ego makes him miss the real threat

jokes.text: Make it gaudy. I'm going clubbing later.
jokes.punchline: Specific style request for social plans
jokes.explanation: Pure Zapp vanity - treating military honors as fashion accessories

jokes.text: Well, Kif, stand by to take the blame.
jokes.punchline: Zapp immediately prepares to scapegoat Kif
jokes.explanation: Classic Zapp-Kif dynamic - immediate blame shifting is perfectly in character

jokes.text: It's a lesson, I say!
jokes.punchline: Treating oppression as educational
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Zoidberg's misguided good intentions

jokes.text: I'm no good at being a slave. I'm thinking about graduate school to become a barber.
jokes.punchline: Random career pivot to barber school
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry logic - completely misses the point of their situation

jokes.text: This can't go on. Today is the day we fight back! It's already 10:00. Oh, you're right. Tomorrow is the day we fight back.
jokes.punchline: Immediate postponement for trivial reason
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of heroic moment - the mundane excuse undercuts the rebellion

jokes.text: Hey, wait. I'm having one of those things. You know, a headache with pictures. An idea? Mm. Mm-mm-mm.
jokes.punchline: Can't remember the word 'idea', then dismisses it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry stupidity - can't even identify having an idea, then loses it

jokes.text: The Bender of the 15th century!
jokes.setup: Old Man describing crossbows as elegant killing machines
jokes.punchline: Bender takes it as personal compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender ego moment - he takes pride in being compared to a weapon

jokes.text: But with the mobile oppression palace... a few dignitaries can oppress your entire planet for pennies a day!
jokes.punchline: Frames oppression like an infomercial for efficiency
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark satire - treating oppression like a business efficiency product

jokes.text: You can crush me, but you can't crush my spirit! Oh, my spirit!
jokes.punchline: Immediately has spirit crushed
jokes.explanation: Classic setup-punchline with perfect timing - the immediate contradiction is the joke

jokes.text: Another victim of the mano-centric male-ocracy.
jokes.punchline: Leela's feminist interpretation with pun
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that's in character for Leela's feminist perspective

jokes.text: I request a satanic funeral.
jokes.punchline: Random request for inappropriate funeral type
jokes.explanation: Random Zoidberg weirdness - the inappropriateness is the joke

jokes.text: Is it possible that all this slavery and oppression... is schmutzing up our freedom lesson? Take a pill, Zoidberg.
jokes.punchline: Ambassador's dismissive response to legitimate concern
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Zoidberg's growing doubt versus authority's dismissiveness

jokes.text: You didn't expect us to even go to a museum... much less steal this ancient heat-seeking missile! I don't even know you.
jokes.setup: Fry's triumphant declaration about their plan
jokes.punchline: Bender's complete disavowal
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic - Bender immediately abandons Fry when things get serious

jokes.text: I don't even know you.
jokes.punchline: Complete dismissal and confusion about who she is
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of heroic moment. Maximum economy with devastating dismissal.

jokes.text: All Decapodian technology is cold-blooded, like us!
jokes.punchline: Explains their technology matches their biology
jokes.explanation: Clever world-building joke - their biology determines their technology design

jokes.text: Hey, I need that to smoke!
jokes.punchline: Bender's selfish concern even during heroic moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender priorities - personal comfort over saving the world

jokes.text: Yes, I'm desecrating a flag, but to preserve the freedom it represents.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg about to burn flag to guide missile
jokes.punchline: Perfectly articulates the paradox of his situation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character arc completion - Zoidberg finally understands true freedom through sacrifice

jokes.text: Wait a second. They do! Because this is my home planet!
jokes.setup: Wishing his home planet appreciated freedom
jokes.punchline: Realizes Earth has become his true home
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character moment - Zoidberg's emotional journey reaches its peak with this realization

jokes.text: Well, maybe just a taste.
jokes.setup: Nixon offering him to bite the flag for photos
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg agrees despite everything that's happened
jokes.explanation: Nice ironic ending - after all the consequences, he's invited to do it again

jokes.text: I wonder what the Shroud of Turin tastes like.
jokes.punchline: Bender wondering about eating religious artifacts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender ending - escalates the concept to even more inappropriate territory

tmdb_overview: Zoidberg becomes the center of a Supreme Court case after he celebrates Freedom Day by publicly eating the Earth flag.
